behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is local parsing/validation logic (range checks, tokenization, syntax), not a cross-module handoff requirement in the behavior text. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: N/A (Unit is lowest). REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is only parser correctness + error message content, with no required module boundary, OS/network/subprocess, or model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven parser tests calling parse() directly with invalid inputs (`13` month, `32` day, malformed fields) and asserting structured error type + message includes offending field/value.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday semantics.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is vulnerable at parserâ†’schedule-objectâ†’calculator handoff for special cron semantics (`L`, `W`-style meaning). REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed; deterministic calendar math in-process is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): a Unit test of calculator with hand-crafted schedule objects can pass while real parsed objects encode edge flags differently, yielding wrong timestamps. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require real external infra; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test from raw cron expression input through parser + calculator, using fixed start times and asserting exact next N UTC timestamps for normal and edge cases (month-end rollover, nearest weekday adjustments).

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules firing within minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure commonly occurs at schedule-expansionâ†’overlap-window boundary (inclusive/exclusive time comparisons, rounding granularity). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): a Unit test of overlap logic on manually supplied timestamps can pass while integrated run-time generation produces boundary timestamps (e.g., exact 5-minute gap) that are interpreted incorrectly. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because no behavior text indicates dependency on real external infra; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser/calculator outputs for two cron schedules into overlap detector, configure a 5-minute threshold, and assert returned conflict windows exactly match expected start/end boundaries at edge cases (equal-to-threshold, just-under-threshold, recurring collisions).

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gaps and fall-back ambiguity for 2:30 AM jobs.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: correctness depends on real timezone/DST resolution behavior (nonexistent/ambiguous local times) from actual runtime tzdata/OS zoneinfo rules, which in-process fakes can mask. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timezone conversion can always encode expected mapping and miss production DST fold/gap handling differences. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Integration): integration tests with stubbed timezone provider may pass while real zoneinfo resolves fall-back ambiguity to a different offset or skips spring-forward times differently. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic scheduling logic with no AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: System test using real timezone database (e.g., `America/New_York`) and real datetime conversion path: schedule `2:30 AM` around concrete DST transition dates, assert engine policy for nonexistent spring time and duplicated fall time matches spec (which occurrence or skip/backfill rule).

behaviors.description: When next run is in the past after downtime, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at handoff between next-run computation, persisted job state/policy, and dispatch decision logic. REAL-INFRA: local in-process state store/fake clock is enough; no real network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): a Unit test of policy function can pass while integrated engine reads stale policy/defaults or combines last-run + now incorrectly, causing wrong skip/backfill action. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess behavior; rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with scheduler engine modules wired together, fixed clock, and persisted job metadata. Simulate downtime so computed next run is past due; assert emitted decision/action sequence differs correctly for `skip` vs `backfill` policies and updates next state accordingly.
